pilot_note: User-supplied category 'DSM' corrected to actual DEV. Closest match WIDAI-DEV-0058 'AI Research Scientist' (key_variants: ML Researcher, Applied Researcher).
strm_coverage: None — O*NET STRM-001 uses content model element IDs (1.A.1.a.1 format), not occupation codes (15-1221.00). STRM not queryable by occupation. Web research primary.

records.ksa_statement_excerpt: Knowledge of supervised and unsupervised machine learning algorithms, including their mathematical foundations, hyperpar
records.web_evidence: 2025-2026 AI Research Scientist / ML Research Scientist JDs: PhD in CS/AI/ML preferred, Python + PyTorch/TensorFlow required, deep learning architectures, NLP, computer vision, transformer models, experimental design, research paper publication, statistical inference, ML evaluation frameworks, dataset curation, model benchmarking, cross-team collaboration. Sources: Adaface, Coursera, Upwork, Second Talent, InterviewGuy. 3+ years research experience standard. Specific: ML algorithm knowledge (supervised, unsupervised, deep learning) universally required in all RS JDs.
records.inclusion_rationale: Supervised and unsupervised ML algorithms are the foundational knowledge for any ML researcher.
records.adversarial_challenge: Too broad — researchers specialize.
records.adversarial_resolution: Foundational ML algorithms are required for all AI researchers regardless of specialization.

records.web_evidence: 2025-2026 AI Research Scientist / ML Research Scientist JDs: PhD in CS/AI/ML preferred, Python + PyTorch/TensorFlow required, deep learning architectures, NLP, computer vision, transformer models, experimental design, research paper publication, statistical inference, ML evaluation frameworks, dataset curation, model benchmarking, cross-team collaboration.
